name: United States
full: United States — DOE, FERC & RTO/ISO Innovation
sub: The US has the largest grid modernisation budget globally, with $62B+ in federal funding through BIL and IRA. FERC Order 2023 creates urgent demand for interconnection automation.
desc: Federal funding through DOE's Grid Deployment Office ($20.5B), ARPA-E ($420M/yr), and SBIR ($4B+ across agencies) provides massive scale. FERC Order 2023 mandates cluster study reforms creating market demand for AI queue management. RTOs/ISOs like PJM, MISO, CAISO, and ERCOT are actively seeking technology partners. A US subsidiary ($1.5-3K) unlocks all federal programmes.

studies.label: DOE Grid Resilience & Innovation Partnerships (GRIP)
studies.text:
What it doesGRIP is the largest single grid modernisation programme in US history, funded by the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law. $10.5B across three tracks: Grid Resilience Grants, Smart Grid Grants, and Grid Innovation Program. In October 2024, $4.2B was awarded to 46 projects across 47 states.Why it mattersSmart Grid Grants specifically fund AI-powered grid management, predictive analytics, and automation. Requires a US-based entity.
DOE GRIP PortalGRIP Projects

eligibility.text: Requires US-based entity. UK startup needs Delaware LLC or C-Corp ($1.5-3K setup, 1-2 weeks). Must partner with US utility.

relevance.text: Smart Grid Grants track specifically funds AI-powered grid management, predictive analytics, and automation. Largest grid modernisation programme in US history.

applicationRoute.detail: Delaware LLC or C-Corp, $1.5-3K setup
Required for all federal funding applications
Smart Grid Grants require utility co-applicant
Watch energy.gov/gdo for funding announcements
$10.5B across three tracks

studies.label: ARPA-E (Advanced Research Projects Agency - Energy)
studies.text:
What it doesARPA-E funds high-risk, high-reward energy technology R&D. Annual budget ~$420M (FY2024-25). Relevant programmes include PERFORM (grid reliability analytics) and open topic calls. Awards typically $1M-$10M over 2-3 years. ~3% success rate.Why it mattersEnormous prestige and credibility. AI-powered grid automation fits their transformative mandate. Requires a US entity.
ARPA-E eXCHANGEPERFORM Programme

eligibility.text: Requires US entity. ~3% success rate. High-risk high-reward mandate. Must demonstrate transformative potential.

relevance.text: AI-powered grid automation fits ARPA-E's transformative mandate. PERFORM programme directly targets grid reliability analytics.

applicationRoute.step: Establish US entity if not already
applicationRoute.detail: Required for application
Relevant programmes include PERFORM and open topics

applicationRoute.step: Submit concept paper when FOA opens
applicationRoute.detail: 3-5 page summary of proposed innovation
Detailed technical plan, budget, and milestones
2-3 year project with quarterly reporting

studies.label: SBIR/STTR (Small Business Innovation Research)
studies.text:
What it doesLargest small business R&D programme in the world. DOE SBIR alone awards ~$300M+ annually. Phase I: up to ~$275K for 6-12 months. Phase II: up to ~$1.1M for 2 years. Phase III: commercialisation (no limit).Why it mattersNon-dilutive funding with clear concept-to-commercialisation pathway. DOE topics frequently include grid modernisation and AI for energy.
SBIR ApplyDOE SBIR/STTRSAM.gov Registration

eligibility.text: Requires US small business entity. DOE SBIR alone awards ~$300M+ annually. Clear pathway from concept to commercialisation.

relevance.text: DOE topics frequently include grid modernisation and AI for energy. Non-dilutive funding with Phase I/II/III commercialisation pathway.

applicationRoute.detail: Must be 51%+ US-owned or controlled
Both required for submission
Watch for grid modernisation and AI energy topics
Up to ~$275K for 6-12 month feasibility study
Up to ~$1.1M for 2-year prototype development

studies.label: FERC Order 2023 — Market Demand Driver
studies.text:
What it doesFERC Order 2023 fundamentally reforms US interconnection: mandatory cluster studies, financial readiness requirements, increased deposits, strict timelines. All RTOs/ISOs implementing by 2025-2026. ~2,000 GW interconnection queue backlog.Individual RTO/ISO Interconnection Portals
PJM Interconnection QueueMISO Generator InterconnectionCAISO InterconnectionSPP InterconnectionERCOT Resource IntegrationISO-NE InterconnectionNYISO InterconnectionsFERC Order 2023 Explainer

eligibility.text: Market demand driver — no application needed. Creates commercial opportunity as RTOs/ISOs seek automation solutions.

relevance.text: Single biggest market driver for AI grid interconnection products. Mandates cluster studies creating massive computational demand across all RTOs/ISOs. ~2000 GW queue backlog.

applicationRoute.detail: PJM, MISO, CAISO are highest priority
Frame around cluster study automation and queue management
Commercial relationship, not grant application
Integration with existing interconnection study processes

studies.label: IRA Section 48C Advanced Energy Manufacturing Tax Credit
studies.text:
What it does30% tax credit for investments in facilities manufacturing clean energy equipment. $10B total with $4B reserved for energy communities. Round 2 (Jan 2025): $6B to 140+ projects. Software companies building AI infrastructure for grid management may qualify.
DOE 48C PortalIRS 48C Credit

eligibility.text: Requires US entity. Software companies building AI infrastructure for grid management may qualify but primary focus is manufacturing.

relevance.text: Primarily targets manufacturing facilities, not software. May qualify if building physical AI infrastructure but not a natural fit for SaaS grid tools.

applicationRoute.detail: Software-only companies may not qualify
Describe qualifying investment and energy impact
Technical and financial assessment

applicationRoute.step: Claim 30% tax credit on qualifying investment
applicationRoute.detail: Applied to facility costs, not operating expenses

studies.label: National Labs Partnership (NREL, PNNL, ANL)
studies.text:
What it doesDOE National Laboratories offer CRADAs, licensing, and Work-for-Others agreements. Labs have deep expertise in power systems modelling and AI for grid operations. International companies can participate though US companies have statutory preference.
NREL Working With UsPNNL Partnerships

eligibility.text: CRADAs available to international companies, though US companies have statutory preference. No US entity strictly required.

relevance.text: NREL and PNNL have deep expertise in power systems modelling and AI for grid operations. Collaboration validates technology with credible institutions.

applicationRoute.step: Contact NREL or PNNL Technology Transfer Office
applicationRoute.detail: Identify relevant research division and point of contact
CRADA, licensing, or Work-for-Others agreement
US companies have preference but international participation possible
Access to lab facilities, data, and domain expertise

faq.q: Do I need a US entity to access federal funding?
faq.a: Yes, for almost all programmes. SBIR requires a US small business. DOE grants require US-based organisations. A Delaware LLC costs $1.5-3K and takes 1-2 weeks.

faq.q: What does FERC Order 2023 mean for our product?
faq.a: It's the single biggest market driver. Mandates cluster studies instead of serial processing, creating massive computational demands. Every RTO/ISO is implementing it and actively seeking automation solutions.

Extremely — roughly 3% success rate. However, the prestige and funding level ($1M-$10M) make it worth pursuing. Frame your application around the step-change in interconnection processing speed that AI enables.

faq.q: Can I partner with a National Lab without a US entity?
faq.a: Yes, CRADAs are available to international companies, though US companies have statutory preference. NREL and PNNL both have international partnership experience.

faq.q: Which RTO/ISO should I target first?
faq.a: PJM (largest US RTO, massive queue) and MISO (facing worst backlog with multi-year study delays) are the highest-priority targets. Both are actively implementing FERC Order 2023 reforms.

note: The US represents the largest market opportunity. FERC Order 2023 creates regulatory urgency. A Delaware LLC ($1.5-3K) unlocks access to the entire federal funding ecosystem including $10.5B GRIP and $4B+ SBIR.
